violations.description: Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Food removed from original packaging was not placed in a container with an impervious and nonabsorbent cover. -An open can of Italian Cut Green Beans were found stored in the refrigerator, uncovered, and not dated.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned adequately. -Cupboards doors and shelves above the three sink system for the kitchen is in need of cleaning.

Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not stored in a clean, dry manner to protect from contamination. -Clean utensils stored in a plastic storage bin have food particles in the bin. Suggest cleaning storage unit.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. -The walls, cupboard doors, and ceilings are all covered in a brown residue and should be deep cleaned. -Grease has pooled up under the fryers and being contained by a cloth at the time of inspection.--Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…) were not maintained in good repair. -Both dry storage rooms have ceiling tiles and insulation falling down. This should be fixed.

Food supplies. — --Food item obtained from unapproved source. *Discovered a box of individually wrapped beef steaks in the walk in cooler that were vacuum sealed and were not labeled or marked were they were processed. Raw meat products must be labeled where they were processed.

Preventing contamination of packaged and unpackaged food. — --Raw animal food was not properly separated from raw or cooked ready-to-eat food. * A box of chips were on the lower shelf of the walk in cooler and raw beef was stored on the shelf above it. Maintain food protection by storing food items according to their risk. Poultry, Beef/Pork and ready to eat foods must be separated.

Thawing potentially hazardous food. — --Potentially hazardous food was not thawed in an approved manner. *Found 2 rolls of partially frozen hamburger thawing on the counter. We discussed proper methods of thawing.

Date marking and disposition of ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food. — --Refrigerated, ready-to-eat potentially hazardous food (prepared more than 24 hours ago) was not properly date marked. * Hamburger patties in the single door upright refrigerator were not date marked.

Reduced-oxygen packaging. — --Establishment using reduced-oxygen process was not properly labeling the food packages. *Discovered individually wrapped vacuum sealed beef steaks that were not date marked or labeled.
Cleaning frequency of equipment and utensils. — --Nonfood contact surface was not cleaned adequately. *The wire rack shelving on the side of the stairs have a build up of grime and dirt.

Storage of cleaned equipment and utensils. — --Clean equipment/utensil was not stored in a clean, dry manner to protect from contamination. * Plate and utensil storage on the wire racking next to the stairs are not covered, stored upright and exposed to dirty wire rack and and unfinished ceiling.

Design and construction of floors, walls, and ceilings. — --Exposed studs, joists or rafters were not covered with smooth, nonabsorbent and easily cleanable material. -Missing ceiling tiles above the utensil and food storage in the stairwell are exposing the utensils and food to an unfinished ceiling.

Cleaning and maintenance of floors, walls and ceilings. — --Physical facilities (e.g. floors, walls, ceiling etc…)* were not clean or not cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. * the floors have a build up of grease and were dirty. Clean as needed and maintain.
Design, construction, clean, maintained (1 pt deducted)
Date marking accomplished, discarded (2 pts deducted)
Cleaning frequency, maintained clean (1 pt deducted)
